symptoms.symptom_name: Insidious chronic lower back pain over 6 months with acute flare

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Insidious onset before age 45 and duration over 3 months are components of the inflammatory back pain criteria for axial spondyloarthritis.
Chronicity over 6 months is atypical for a simple mechanical strain, which is usually self-limiting.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prominent morning stiffness that eases with movement and worsens with rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Improvement with exercise but not rest, and marked morning stiffness, are core discriminators of inflammatory (vs mechanical) back pain.
Mechanical pain characteristically worsens with movement and eases with rest — the opposite pattern to this presentation.
Enthesitis, particularly at the Achilles insertion, is a hallmark extra-articular feature of spondyloarthritis.
Fatigue is a common and often disabling systemic feature of active spondyloarthritis, reflecting inflammatory disease activity.
Acute severe immobilising back pain is a trigger to actively screen for cauda equina, though immobility alone is non-specific and more often reflects a severe inflammatory flare.
A severe flare of inflammatory back pain can cause transient functional immobility from pain and stiffness.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal bladder and bowel control (no sphincter disturbance)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved bladder and bowel function substantially lowers the probability of cauda equina syndrome, though it does not fully exclude an early or evolving presentation without examination.
Absence of saddle anaesthesia on directed history argues strongly against cauda equina compression; formal confirmation requires perianal sensory examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: No radiating leg pain and no new leg weakness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of dermatomal radiating leg pain and motor weakness reduces the likelihood of a compressive radiculopathy.
Preserved lower-limb power and absence of bilateral radicular pain lower cauda equina probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, night sweats or weight loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of constitutional features argues against spinal infection or malignancy as the cause of the acute flare, supporting a non-sinister inflammatory process by exclusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced lumbar flexion on modified Schober test with sacroiliac tenderness (examination sign)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Restricted lumbar flexion and sacroiliac joint tenderness support active axial spondyloarthritis, but these are clinician examination findings not obtainable via telehealth chat.
HLA-B27 positivity and raised CRP/ESR increase the probability of axial spondyloarthritis and support classification, but require pathology testing not available in a telehealth chat.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of insidious chronic low back pain, inflammatory-type morning stiffness that eases with movement, bilateral Achilles enthesitis and systemic fatigue constitutes a coherent inflammatory back pain / spondyloarthritis pattern that is far more powerful than any single feature.

Preserved sphincter function, normal saddle sensation and absence of new leg weakness together make cauda equina unlikely, but because none can be examined remotely they define the mandatory safety-net rather than a definitive exclusion.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: MRI of sacroiliac joints and lumbar spine
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: MRI is required to demonstrate active sacroiliitis and confirm non-radiographic axial spondyloarthritis, and to exclude compressive or sinister causes. Arranged via GP/rheumatology, not obtainable in telehealth.
Supports classification of axial spondyloarthritis when positive; arranged through the GP as part of the rheumatological workup.
Inflammatory markers help gauge disease activity and screen for infective causes; arranged via GP pathology.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate spinal MRI ordered directly from the telehealth consult
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Emergency MRI is only indicated if cauda equina or a sinister cause is suspected — the neurological screen here is reassuring, so demanding an urgent MRI from telehealth over-escalates a presumptive inflammatory flare. Urgent imaging becomes appropriate only if a red flag develops.
